methods.description: This method describes the clinical characterization of ragweed-allergic patients, including the assessment of symptoms and sensitization to other allergens.

steps.description: The study cohort comprises 150 ragweed-allergic patients (100 males and 50 females) aged between 18 and 61 years.
steps.searchable_summary: collect demographic data of 150 ragweed-allergic patients

steps.description: All included patients reported rhinitis symptoms, 93% complained of conjunctivitis, 63% of asthma-like symptoms and 26% of skin symptoms upon ragweed pollen exposure.
steps.searchable_summary: process symptom data for ragweed-allergic patients

steps.description: Sensitization to other common allergen sources was assessed by clinical history, positive SPT and/or serological IgE tests using allergen extracts.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sensitization to other allergens using clinical history and IgE tests

steps.description: The prevalence of symptoms reported upon ragweed pollen exposure and other sensitizations are displayed in Table 1.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze and display symptom prevalence and sensitization data

steps.description: Table S1 shows the specific IgE (sIgE) levels against ragweed pollen extract, nAmb a 1, nArt v 1, rBet v 1, rPhl p 1/5b and CCD for 150 patients included in our study cohort and displayed in Figure 1.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze specific IgE levels for various allergens
